title: microcos phaneroneura
intro: Microcos phaneroneura, also known as the "golden cup flower," is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions of Asia. It is a species of shrub that belongs to the family Malvaceae, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Microcos phaneroneura is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to provide relief from various health issues, including fever, headache, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used to promote overall health and well-being due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects.
